verses.1: My son, if you have become a guarantor for your neighbor, Or have given a handshake for a stranger,
verses.2: If you have been ensnared by the words of your mouth, Or caught by the words of your mouth,
verses.3: Then do this, my son, and save yourself: Since you have come into the hand of your neighbor, Go, humble yourself, and be urgent with your neighbor to free yourself.
verses.4: Give no sleep to your eyes, Nor slumber to your eyelids;
verses.5: Save yourself like a gazelle from the hunter’s hand, And like a bird from the hand of the fowler.
verses.6: Go to the ant, you lazy one, Observe its ways and be wise,
verses.7: Which, having no chief, Officer, or ruler,
verses.8: Prepares its food in the summer And gathers its provision in the harvest.
verses.9: How long will you lie down, you lazy one? When will you arise from your sleep?
verses.10: “A little sleep, a little slumber, A little folding of the hands to rest,”
verses.11: Then your poverty will come in like a drifter, And your need like an armed man.
verses.12: A worthless person, a wicked man, Is one who walks with a perverse mouth,
verses.13: Who winks with his eyes, who signals with his feet, Who points with his fingers;
verses.14: Who, with perversion in his heart, continually devises evil, Who spreads strife.
verses.15: Therefore his disaster will come suddenly; Instantly he will be broken and there will be no healing.
verses.16: There are six things that the LORD hates, Seven that are an abomination to Him:
verses.17: Haughty eyes, a lying tongue, And hands that shed innocent blood,
verses.18: A heart that devises wicked plans, Feet that run rapidly to evil,
verses.19: A false witness who declares lies, And one who spreads strife among brothers.
verses.20: My son, comply with the commandment of your father, And do not ignore the teaching of your mother;
verses.21: Bind them continually on your heart; Tie them around your neck.
verses.22: When you walk, they will guide you; When you sleep, they will watch over you; And when you awake, they will talk to you.
verses.23: For the commandment is a lamp and the teaching is light; And rebukes for discipline are the way of life
verses.24: To keep you from the evil woman, From the smooth tongue of the foreign woman.
verses.25: Do not desire her beauty in your heart, Nor let her capture you with her eyelids.
verses.26: For the price of a prostitute reduces one to a loaf of bread, And an adulteress hunts for a precious life.
verses.27: Can anyone take fire in his lap And his clothes not be burned?
verses.28: Or can a person walk on hot coals And his feet not be scorched?
verses.29: So is the one who goes in to his neighbor’s wife; Whoever touches her will not go unpunished.
verses.30: People do not despise a thief if he steals To satisfy himself when he is hungry;
verses.31: But when he is found, he must repay seven times as much; He must give up all the property of his house.
verses.32: One who commits adultery with a woman is lacking sense; He who would destroy himself commits it.
verses.33: He will find wounds and disgrace, And his shame will not be removed.
verses.34: For jealousy enrages a man, And he will not have compassion on the day of vengeance.
verses.35: He will not accept any settlement, Nor will he be satisfied though you make it a large gift.
